Home / Services / Subfloor Services

Subfloor Services

Expert subfloor leveling and repairs for a sturdy, lasting foundation.

A solid subfloor is the unsung hero of any successful flooring installation. Without a properly leveled and structurally sound subfloor, even the most beautiful hardwood, tile, or vinyl flooring will eventually succumb to problems like squeaks, cracks, and unevenness. NextGen Floors LLC provides comprehensive subfloor services to ensure a stable and durable foundation for your new or existing floors in the Baltimore area.

We specialize in addressing the unique challenges posed by Baltimore's older homes, many of which have uneven or damaged subfloors due to settling, moisture, or age. Our team is skilled in leveling subfloors, repairing damaged joists, and installing new subfloor panels to create a smooth and solid base for your finished flooring.

Whether you're planning a new flooring installation or simply need to address existing subfloor problems, we have the expertise to get the job done right. We use advanced leveling techniques and high-quality materials to ensure a long-lasting and trouble-free result.

Our Process

  1. 01 Inspection and Assessment
  2. 02 Leveling the Subfloor
  3. 03 Reinforcing and Repairing
  4. 04 Subfloor Replacement

Benefits

  • Prevents Flooring Issues
  • Provides a Stable Base
  • Extends Floor Life
  • Increases Home Value

Frequently Asked Questions

Why is subfloor preparation important?

Proper subfloor preparation is crucial for ensuring the longevity and performance of your finished flooring. An uneven or damaged subfloor can lead to a variety of problems, including squeaks, cracks, and unevenness.

How do you level a subfloor?

We use a variety of leveling techniques, including self-leveling compounds, shims, and plywood overlays, to create a smooth and level surface. The best method depends on the specific conditions of your subfloor.

What are the signs of subfloor damage?

Common signs of subfloor damage include squeaky floors, soft spots, uneven surfaces, and visible cracks. You may also notice musty odors or signs of moisture damage.
